title: metrosideros rotundifolia
intro: Metrosideros rotundifolia, also known as the red rātā or red flower tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Metrosideros rotundifolia include its ability to help reduce fever, alleviate colds and flu, and soothe skin irritations. Metrosideros rotundifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in modern medicine to develop new treatments for various health conditions.
